extDescription.message: Sets unread email count on the installed Outlook app icon using the Badging API.

optionsDisclaimerText.message: Only folders listed in Favorites are included in badge calculation. Folders outside Favorites are ignored, and custom include/exclude rules apply only to folders that are in Favorites.

optionsTitleCounterDescription.message: Show unread count in Outlook page title (for example: (2) Mail - ...).

optionsShowUnreadInPageTitle.message: Show unread count in page title

optionsAppBadgeDescription.message: Show unread count on Outlook app icon badge. By default, this is disabled on Windows.

optionsShowUnreadOnAppIcon.message: Show unread count on app icon

optionsBuiltInExclusionsDescription.message: Disable any default exclusion rule below. When enabled, that folder type is excluded from badge counting. These built-in rules are language-agnostic.

optionsCustomRulesDescription.message: Add exact folder name and choose whether to include or exclude it. Rules are evaluated only for folders present in Favorites; custom include rules override built-in exclusions only for that exact folder name (case-insensitive, diacritics-sensitive).

optionsDiagnosticsDescription.message: Current settings and storage health information.

optionsSyncFallbackWarning.message: Sync write failed, using local storage fallback.

optionsResetDescription.message: Reset all extension settings to defaults.

optionsHeaderDescription.message: Counts unread messages from Favorites for app icon badge and page title.

optionsMcasDescription.message: If your organization routes Outlook through Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (the address ends with mcas.ms), enable this so the extension can run there. Your browser will ask for permission for outlook.cloud.microsoft.mcas.ms only.

optionsDisclaimerMcasHint.message: Work or school account? If your Outlook address ends with .mcas.ms, your organization routes it through a security proxy — enable proxy support in the "Defender for Cloud Apps (MCAS) Support" section below.

popupMcasBannerText.message: It looks like your organization runs Outlook through a security proxy (the address ends with .mcas.ms). Enable proxy support for the badge to work.

popupMcasEnabledConfirmation.message: Proxy support enabled — reload your Outlook tab.
